q.en: Why is Pi Day celebrated on March 14?
q.zh: 为什么圆周率日在3月14日庆祝？

options.en: Because Congress chose a random spring date in 2009.
options.zh: 因为国会在2009年随机选了一个春天的日期。

options.en: Because in the U.S. date format (month/day/year), March 14 is written 3-14, the first three digits of pi.
options.zh: 因为美国日期写法（月/日/年）中，3月14日写作3-14，正好是圆周率的前三位数字。

options.en: Because March 14 is the day the Greek mathematician Archimedes was born. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 因为3月14日是希腊数学家阿基米德的生日。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Because a circle has exactly 314 degrees.
options.zh: 因为一个圆正好有314度。

explain.en: The book states people chose March 14 because of the U.S. date format: March is the 3rd month and the 14th day gives '3-14,' the first three digits of pi.
explain.zh: 书中说，人们选择3月14日是因为美国的日期写法：三月是第3个月，14日是第14天，合起来是“3-14”，即圆周率的前三位数字。

q.en: According to the book, who was the FIRST person to give the special number the name 'pi'?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，谁是第一个给这个特殊数字起名“pi”的人？

options.en: Albert Einstein, who was born on Pi Day
options.zh: 在圆周率日出生的阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦

options.en: Archimedes, the Greek mathematician who discovered it according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 发现它的希腊数学家阿基米德（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A U.S. Congress representative in 2009
options.zh: 2009年的一位美国国会议员

options.en: William Jones, a math teacher in England, in 1706
options.zh: 1706年英格兰一位数学老师威廉·琼斯

explain.en: The book says, 'In 1706, a math teacher in England named William Jones was the first to call this special number pi.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“1706年，英格兰一位名叫威廉·琼斯的数学老师第一个把这特殊的数字称为 pi。”

q.en: What world record related to pi is mentioned in the book?
q.zh: 书中提到了哪项与圆周率有关的世界纪录？

options.en: An American student wrote out pi to 1,120 places.
options.zh: 一名美国学生把圆周率写到了第1,120位。

options.en: A computer calculated pi to exactly one million digits.
options.zh: 一台计算机把圆周率精确计算到了一百万位。

options.en: Marc Umile memorized 15,314 digits, the all-time record. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: Marc Umile记住了15,314位数字，是历史最高纪录。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Chao Lu of China recited pi from memory to 67,890 places in November 2006.
options.zh: 中国的Chao Lu在2006年11月凭记忆把圆周率背到了第67,890位。

explain.en: The 'Wowser!' section says Chao Lu from China recited pi from memory to 67,890 places in November 2006.
explain.zh: 书中“Wowser!”部分写道，来自中国的Chao Lu在2006年11月凭记忆背诵圆周率到了第67,890位。

q.en: In the book's glossary, what does the word 'circumference' mean?
q.zh: 在书的词汇表中，“circumference”是什么意思？

options.en: The distance across a circle through its middle
options.zh: 从圆的一侧穿过中心到另一侧的距离

options.en: The distance around the outside of a circle
options.zh: 圆外围一圈的距离

options.en: The amount of space inside a flat shape
options.zh: 一个平面图形内部所占的空间

options.en: The amount of space inside a three-dimensional shape
options.zh: 一个三维形状内部所占的空间

explain.en: The glossary defines circumference as 'the distance around the outside (n.) of a circle.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将circumference定义为“圆周（名词），即圆外围一圈的距离”。

options.en: The distance around a circle
options.zh: 圆周的长度

options.en: The first three digits of pi according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 圆周率的前三位数字（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The amount of space something takes up, measured in cubic units
options.zh: 某物所占的空间，以立方单位计量

options.en: A kind of mathematician
options.zh: 一种数学家的称呼

explain.en: The glossary defines volume as 'the amount of space that something takes up as measured in cubic units.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将volume定义为“体积（名词），即某物所占的空间，以立方单位计量”。

q.en: How do landscapers use pi, according to the book?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，园林工人如何使用圆周率？

options.en: To figure out how much area a lawn sprinkler can cover, so they know how many sprinklers to use
options.zh: 用来算出草坪洒水器能覆盖的面积，从而知道需要准备多少个洒水器

options.en: To decide which type of grass to plant
options.zh: 用来决定种哪种草

options.en: To measure the water pressure of a sprinkler
options.zh: 用来测量洒水器的水压

options.en: To count how many blades of grass are in a lawn according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 用来数草坪上有多少根草（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says landscapers use pi 'to figure out how much area a lawn sprinkler can cover. That amount tells them how many sprinklers they need in order to water a lawn evenly.'
explain.zh: 书中说，园林工人用圆周率来“算出草坪洒水器能覆盖的面积。这个数字告诉他们，要让草坪均匀浇水需要准备多少个洒水器”。

q.en: What makes a 'once-in-a-century' Pi Day special, as described in the book?
q.zh: 书中描述的“百年一遇”的圆周率日特殊在哪里？

options.en: It happens every 100 years on a leap day.
options.zh: 它每100年在闰日发生一次。

options.en: It is the day the U.S. Congress made Pi Day official. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 那是美国国会正式确立圆周率日的日子。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: It falls on 3-14-15, and people can start at 9:26:53 AM to add two more digits (3.141592653).
options.zh: 它落在3-14-15，人们可以在上午9:26:53开始庆祝，从而再多两位数字（3.141592653）。

options.en: It is Albert Einstein's birthday.
options.zh: 那天是阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦的生日。

explain.en: The book explains that once a century there is a special Pi Day—3-14-15—and people can start the celebration at 9:26:53 AM to add two more digits, 3.141592653.
explain.zh: 书中解释说，每个世纪会有一次特殊的圆周率日——3-14-15——人们可以在上午9:26:53开始庆祝，从而再多两位数字：3.141592653。

q.en: Why does the author say people around the world celebrate Pi Day?
q.zh: 作者说全世界的人们为什么庆祝圆周率日？

options.en: To remember that science can be fun as well as useful, and because pi is important for math and science
options.zh: 为了记住科学既实用又有趣，而且圆周率对数学和科学十分重要

options.en: To honor the inventor of the calendar according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了向历法的发明者致敬（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Because pie is the national food
options.zh: 因为派是国民食物

options.en: To raise money for schools
options.zh: 为了给学校筹款

explain.en: The closing says, 'It helps us remember that science can be fun as well as useful.'
explain.zh: 书的结尾写道：“它让我们记住，科学既实用又有趣。”

q.en: How did the very first Pi Day celebration begin?
q.zh: 第一个圆周率日庆祝活动是如何开始的？

options.en: In 2009, when the U.S. Congress voted for it according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 2009年，美国国会投票确立时（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: In 1988, a scientist named Shaw arranged a celebration at the San Francisco Exploratorium
options.zh: 1988年，一位名叫Shaw的科学家在旧金山探索馆组织了庆祝活动

options.en: At Albert Einstein's birthday party
options.zh: 在阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦的生日派对上

options.en: In ancient Greece with Archimedes
options.zh: 在古希腊，由阿基米德发起

explain.en: The book says the first Pi Day was in 1988, arranged by a scientist named Shaw at the San Francisco Exploratorium, where people marched, sang, and ate pie.
explain.zh: 书中说，第一个圆周率日是1988年由一位名叫Shaw的科学家在旧金山探索馆（Exploratorium）组织的，人们游行、唱歌并吃派。

q.en: Besides eating pie and pizza, what is another way people celebrate Pi Day mentioned in the book?
q.zh: 除了吃派和披萨，书中还提到人们用哪种方式庆祝圆周率日？

options.en: Walking or running a 'pi marathon' of 3.14 miles or 3.14 kilometers
options.zh: 走或跑一场3.14英里（或3.14公里）的“圆周率马拉松”

options.en: Climbing a mountain 3.14 times
options.zh: 把一座山爬3.14次

options.en: Reading the book aloud for 3.14 hours according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 把这本书大声朗读3.14小时（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Swimming 3.14 laps in a pool
options.zh: 在泳池里游3.14圈

explain.en: The book says some people 'work off their food by walking or running a pi marathon' of 3.14 miles or 3.14 kilometers.
explain.zh: 书中说，有些人会通过“走或跑一场3.14英里（或3.14公里）的圆周率马拉松”来消耗吃下的食物。

prompt.en: Imagine it is Pi Day. Write a short paragraph about how you and your family would celebrate, and explain one real way pi is used in everyday life.
prompt.zh: 想象今天是圆周率日。写一小段话，描述你和家人会怎样庆祝，并解释圆周率在日常生活中的一个真实用途。

en: Include at least one food people eat on Pi Day, such as pie, pizza, or pineapple juice.
Mention a real use of pi from the book, like figuring out a circle's area, a silo's volume, or a sprinkler's coverage.
zh: 至少提到一种人们在圆周率日会吃的食物，比如派、披萨或菠萝汁。
从书中举出一个圆周率的真实用途，比如计算圆的面积、筒仓的体积或洒水器的覆盖面积。
